marketState: neutral-to-mildly-bearish, near-term heat support fading into a storage overhang
summary: Front price ($2.923) is essentially at fair value ($2.917, spread +$0.006) inside a wide $2.417-$3.417 band, while running about 10.8pp/14.2% below its typical seasonal path — a below-median reading whose seasonality signal is explicitly flagged Low reliability (50pp spread) this week, so it shouldn't be leaned on alone. Near-term (1-7D) weather demand is +19.8% above 5-year normal (rangePos 0.88), but that heat is already rolling over (8-16D demand -3.2% vs 5y, 24h trend -5.2), making the bullish weather tailwind look short-lived. Storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build, and managed money is net short (-60,295 contracts, 35th percentile) — both consistent with a supply overhang. Daily/weekly technicals are bearish (RSI ~36-38, price below the lower daily Bollinger band) even as the shortest timeframes (5m/15m/1h) show tentative stabilization, and the futures curve is mildly backwardated out to October before flipping into a large, normal winter contango from November onward. Net read: fundamentals skew mildly bearish for the 3-12 week window (tempered by the low-reliability seasonality flag and typical September seasonal strength inside that window), while the 1-10 day picture favors a possible bounce given daily oversold conditions, fair-value support, and still-elevated near-term heat demand.
